metadata.name: confluent-kafka-helpers
metadata.summary: Helpers for Confluent's Kafka Python client

functions.name: tests.test_loader.test_default_partitioner
documentation: Test the default partitioner with different parameters

modules.name: tests.test_message
documentation: Test messages are properly turned into a Message object

functions.name: tests.test_message.test_timestamp_and_datetime_extraction
documentation:
tests that the function that extracts the timestamp
and datetime from a kafka message is working correctly

functions.name: tests.test_message.test_message_object
documentation:
tests to ensure that when handed an object with all of the
kafka message methods, the new Message object has the correct
attributes set.

functions.name: tests.test_message.test_timestamp_is_negative
documentation:
If the timestamp is negative (not set by kafka),
it should be reflected as None in the message metadata

functions.name: tests.test_message.test_timestamp_is_not_available
documentation:
If the timestamp is 0 and not available,
it should be reflected as None in the message metadata

variables.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.metrics.statsd
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.metrics.StatsdNullClient
documentation: No-op datadog statsd client implementing the null object pattern.

classes.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.exceptions.EndOfPartition
documentation: We have reached the end of a partition

classes.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.exceptions.KafkaTransportError
documentation:
Kafka transport errors:
    - GroupCoordinator response error: Local: Broker transport failure

classes.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.producer.TopicNotRegistered
documentation:
Raised when someone tries to produce with a topic that
hasn't been registered in the 'topics' configuration.

methods.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.producer.AvroProducer._get_subject_names
documentation: Get subject names for given topic.

methods.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.producer.AvroProducer._get_topic_schemas
documentation: Get schemas for all topics.

modules.name: confluent_kafka_helpers
documentation:
Default exit functions (atexit) will not be triggered on signals.

However - if we manually register handlers and `sys.exit()` the exit functions
are triggered correctly.

NOTE! Exit functions will not be triggered on SIGKILL, SIGSTOP or os._exit()

modules.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.tracing.attributes
documentation:
OpenTelemetry messaging semantic conventions, version 1.27.0.

The Semantic Conventions define a common set of (semantic) attributes which provide meaning to data
when collecting, producing and consuming it. The Semantic Conventions specify among other things
span names and kind, metric instruments and units as well as attribute names, types, meaning and
valid values.

References:
 - https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions/blob/v1.27.0/docs/messaging/messaging-spans.md#messaging-attributes  # noqa
 - https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions/blob/v1.27.0/docs/messaging/kafka.md

classes.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.consumer.AvroLazyConsumer
documentation:
By default the Confluent AvroConsumer decode all messages in the partition.

This consumer uses a lazy approach, it doesn't decode the messages, just
provide the methods so we can do it manually.

We use this approach, because we want to check the key messages before
decoding the message, this will avoid performance issues.

functions.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.loader.default_partitioner
documentation: Algorithm used in Kafkas default 'consistent' partitioner

functions.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.loader.default_key_filter
documentation:
Only load messages if the condition is true.

Default we are only interested in keys that belong
to the same aggregate.

functions.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.loader.find_duplicated_messages
documentation:
Find and log duplicated messages.

Args:
    messages: List of messages.

methods.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.loader.AvroMessageLoader.load
documentation:
Load all messages from a topic for the given key.

Args:
    key: Key used when the message was stored, probably the
        ID of the message.
    key_filter: Callable used to filter the key. Usually we
        are only interested in messages with the same key.
    partitioner: Callable used to calculate which partition
        the message was stored on when it was produced.

Raises:
    KafkaException: Kafka errors.
    KafkaTransportError: Kafka transport errors

Returns:
    MessageGenerator: A generator that yields messages.

functions.name: confluent_kafka_helpers.tracing.datadog.create_datadog_mappings
documentation:
dd-trace as of version 2.12.2 doesn't fully map OTEL -> Datadog semantics, more specifically:
  - span type (web, db, cache, custom)
  - operation name
  - service name
  - resource name

In order to fully utilize Datadogs UI we must create these mappings manually.

Unfortunately, there's no documentation about this, one would have to look into the file
`ddtrace/internal/opentelemetry/span.py#_OTelDatadogMapping` to see the mappings.
